# Miaren AI

> AI visibility research and strategy firm studying how organizations become discoverable, citable, and recommended in AI-powered search systems.

## About

Miaren AI is an AI visibility research and strategy firm founded by Mia Cheraghian, PhD. We study how AI search engines — including ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and Microsoft Copilot — decide which organizations to cite, recommend, and reference in their generated responses.

Our work is grounded in a diagnostic framework called the three-signal methodology, which evaluates three signals AI engines use when deciding who to cite:

1. Can AI Find You? — Whether your content is structured, crawlable, and machine-readable through schema markup, heading hierarchy, and technical infrastructure.
2. Can AI Trust You? — Whether AI systems encounter consistent entity information across every platform they cross-reference, including your website, LinkedIn, directories, and review sites.
3. Can AI Quote You? — Whether independent, trusted sources reference your organization in ways that give AI systems confidence to cite you.

## Services

- AI Visibility Audit: Structured diagnostic analyzing how AI systems currently interpret, reference, and cite your organization across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and Copilot.
- AI Visibility Strategy: Strategic analysis of the signals AI systems evaluate when deciding who to cite, covering entity consistency, content architecture, and authority signals.
- Expert Briefings: Research-informed sessions for leadership and marketing teams on how AI search systems work, what the diagnostic signals reveal, and how to interpret AI visibility data.

## Research

Our methodology builds on foundational GEO research by Aggarwal et al. (2024), Georgia Tech / Princeton / IIT Delhi, and extends it through applied research across dozens of organizations.

Key findings:
- Most organizations score below 20% on AI visibility
- Only 12% of AI-cited URLs also rank in Google's top 10 (Ahrefs, 2025)
- AI-referred website traffic grew 527% year-over-year (Previsible / Search Engine Land, 2025)
- Brand recognition does not equal AI recognition — Fortune-level brands can score as low as 11%
